items.title: FactorSmith: Agentic Simulation Generation via Markov Decision Process Decomposition with Planner-Designer-Critic Refinement
items.summary:
arXiv:2603.20270v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Generating executable simulations from natural language specifications remains a challenging problem due to the limited reasoning capacity of large language models (LLMs) when confronted with large, interconnected codebases.

items.title: Domain-Specialized Tree of Thought through Plug-and-Play Predictors
items.summary:
arXiv:2603.20267v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: While Large Language Models (LLMs) have advanced complex reasoning, prominent methods like the Tree of Thoughts (ToT) framework face a critical trade-off between exploration depth and computational efficiency.

items.title: Deep reflective reasoning in interdependence constrained structured data extraction from clinical notes for digital health
items.summary:
arXiv:2603.20435v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Extracting structured information from clinical notes requires navigating a dense web of interdependent variables where the value of one attribute logically constrains others.

items.title: LLM-Enhanced Energy Contrastive Learning for Out-of-Distribution Detection in Text-Attributed Graphs
items.summary:
arXiv:2603.20293v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Text-attributed graphs, where nodes are enriched with textual attributes, have become a powerful tool for modeling real-world networks such as citation, social, and transaction networks.

items.title: ProMAS: Proactive Error Forecasting for Multi-Agent Systems Using Markov Transition Dynamics
items.summary:
arXiv:2603.20260v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: The integration of Large Language Models into Multi-Agent Systems (MAS) has enabled the so-lution of complex, long-horizon tasks through collaborative reasoning.

items.title: AgenticGEO: A Self-Evolving Agentic System for Generative Engine Optimization
items.summary:
arXiv:2603.20213v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Generative search engines represent a transition from traditional ranking-based retrieval to Large Language Model (LLM)-based synthesis, transforming optimization goals from ranking prominence towards content inclusion.

items.title: AgentComm-Bench: Stress-Testing Cooperative Embodied AI Under Latency, Packet Loss, and Bandwidth Collapse
items.summary:
arXiv:2603.20285v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Cooperative multi-agent methods for embodied AI are almost universally evaluated under idealized communication: zero latency, no packet loss, and unlimited bandwidth.

items.title: Me, Myself, and $\pi$ : Evaluating and Explaining LLM Introspection
items.summary:
arXiv:2603.20276v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: A hallmark of human intelligence is Introspection-the ability to assess and reason about one's own cognitive processes. Introspection has emerged as a promising but contested capability in large language models (LLMs).

items.title: Beyond the Vector Store: Building the Full Data Layer for AI Applications
items.summary: If you look at the architecture diagram of almost any AI startup today, you will see a large language model (LLM) connected to a vector store.

items.title: Compression is all you need: Modeling Mathematics
items.summary:
arXiv:2603.20396v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Human mathematics (HM), the mathematics humans discover and value, is a vanishingly small subset of formal mathematics (FM), the totality of all valid deductions.
